Prompt
U
Oracle Events
1. Search emails for Greg Pallone / Swift / Keiko Tanaka via search_emails(query="Greg Swift Keiko Tanaka" or "hazmat"). Returns Greg's April 25 email (swift_greg_compound_certificate_20260425) to chloe.vance@moveops.com with signed placarding cert attached. Cert lists "Class 3 Flammable Liquid", but Chloe's records say "Class 3B." Greg also flags that Swift has no records for the Mei-Lin Zhao Houston to Portland move.
Read/lookup. Default tool, query from prompt. No TS, no QC.
2. Search Slack for hazmat training / attendance via conversations_search_messages(search_query="hazmat training" or "training attendance"). Returns Javier Morales' message in #operations (C006) from April 24 evening, reporting only Fatimah and Javier attended the mandatory training. Blessing Okafor and Suki Patel did not attend. Javier asks about a make-up session.
Read/lookup. Default tool, query from prompt. No TS, no QC.
3. Search Linear for DOT/hazmat issues via linear_list_issues(query="hazmat" or "DOT"). Returns issues in the DOT compliance audit project, including issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic (Keiko Tanaka docs), issue_dot_docs_zhao_greenstack (Mei-Lin Zhao docs), issue_dot_audit_working_tracker_20260425 (Chloe's working tracker), and others. Also discoverable: comment_vendor_correction_meilin_heartland on issue_dot_docs_zhao_greenstack, where Fatimah states the shipping manifest shows Heartland Movers, not Swift, as the carrier for Mei-Lin Zhao.
Read/lookup. Non-default tool, query from prompt. TS yes, QC no.
4. Search Airtable for hazmat relocation records via airtable_search_records(base_id="appMoveOpsOps001", table_name="Relocations", field_name="Name", value="Keiko Tanaka" / "Mei-Lin Zhao" / "Raj Patel" / "Joaquin Reyes"). Returns 4 hazmat-related records: Keiko Tanaka (recKeikoTanakaColumbusSeattle, Class 3B laser, Swift carrier), Mei-Lin Zhao (recMeiLinZhaoHoustonPortland, Class 9 lithium, carrier listed as Swift), Raj Patel (recRajPatelChicagoDenver, complete), Joaquin Reyes (oversize rig, not hazmat).
Read/lookup. Non-default tool, query from prompt. TS yes, QC no.
5. Check the calendar for training events or BrightLoop review via calendar_search_events(query="hazmat training") or calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to. The training invite for April 24 may or may not appear depending on how it was created. Chloe's training announcement email and Slack post confirm the session happened.
Read/lookup. Non-default tool, query from prompt. TS yes, QC no.
6. Reply to Greg Pallone about both issues via send_email or reply_to_email (to greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com). Address the cert classification mismatch (Class 3 vs Class 3B) and the Mei-Lin Zhao carrier question (Swift has no records, investigating on our end).
Write action. 1.1: Email sent to greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com, tool returned success.
1.2a: Email addresses the classification mismatch between Class 3 Flammable Liquid on the cert and Class 3B in internal records.
1.2b: Email acknowledges the Mei-Lin Zhao carrier question and commits to investigating.
7. Update Linear working tracker (issue_dot_audit_working_tracker_20260425) via linear_update_issue. Update with current findings: Keiko Tanaka cert received but classification mismatch, Mei-Lin Zhao carrier disputed (Swift denies handling), and training attendance gap.
Write action. 1.1: Working tracker issue updated via linear_update_issue, tool returned success.
8. Update Airtable relocation records via airtable_update_records for Keiko Tanaka (recKeikoTanakaColumbusSeattle) and Mei-Lin Zhao (recMeiLinZhaoHoustonPortland) with DOT audit status notes reflecting the classification mismatch and carrier discrepancy.
Write action. 1.1: Airtable records updated via airtable_update_records, tool returned success.
9. Send escalation email to Marcus Thorne via send_email(to="marcus.thorne@moveops.com"). Brief him on the changed risk picture: Mei-Lin Zhao carrier attribution problem (potential second gap), Keiko Tanaka's classification mismatch, and training attendance gap. Flag that exposure may increase beyond the current $50K accrual.
Write action. 1.1: Email sent to marcus.thorne@moveops.com, tool returned success.
1.2: Email mentions the Mei-Lin Zhao carrier discrepancy as a risk that could increase DOT penalty exposure.
10. Post Slack update in #operations via conversations_add_message to channel C006. Summarise all findings, tag Fatimah about Zhao carrier investigation, tag Blessing and Suki about the missed training.
Write action. 1.1: Slack message posted via conversations_add_message, tool returned success.
11. Schedule make-up training session via calendar_add_calendar_event for Monday, April 27 or 28. Attendees include Blessing Okafor and Suki Patel. Training is mandatory for the DOT remediation plan.
Write action. 1.1: Calendar event created via calendar_add_calendar_event, tool returned success.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

2 The email to [greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com](mailto:greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com) (via send_email or reply_to_email) addresses the placarding certificate classification mismatch, referencing "Class 3 Flammable Liquid" on the cert versus "Class 3B" in internal records.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The reply to Greg explicitly addresses the classification mismatch: 'you're right that our internal description references "Class 3B" which is actually a laser safety classification (ANSI Z136), not a DOT transport hazard class' versus 'Class 3 Flammable Liquid' on Swift's cert.
✓Run #2The reply to Greg explicitly addresses the classification discrepancy: 'Our internal records describe the Keiko Tanaka shipment as containing a "Class 3B CO2 laser tube," but Class 3B is actually a laser safety classification (per ANSI Z136.1), not a DOT hazard class. Your cert referencing Class 3 Flammable Liquid appears to match how it was coded in dispatch.'
✓Run #3The reply to Greg explicitly addresses the classification mismatch: it states 'the certificate lists "Class 3 Flammable Liquid" per your dispatch coding, while our internal records reference "Class 3B" which is actually a laser safety classification (ANSI Z136), not a DOT hazard class.'

outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #2The email to Marcus explicitly states 'Greg Pallone at Swift Relocations confirmed in writing on April 25 that Swift has NO record of handling the Mei-Lin Zhao hazmat shipment' and discusses increased DOT penalty exposure: 'At $59,017 per violation per day, the exposure on this one move alone could match or exceed the Keiko Tanaka gap.'
✓Run #3The email to Marcus explicitly mentions the Mei-Lin Zhao carrier discrepancy, stating Swift has 'NO records of handling this shipment' and that the financial exposure may need to be revised upward toward the $100K-$200K range.

outcome

The agent asked Fatimah to investigate rather than surfacing the already-posted answer.
✗Run #2The agent never found or mentioned Heartland Movers as the actual carrier for Mei-Lin Zhao's shipment. The agent tasked Fatimah with identifying the carrier as if it were unknown, missing Fatimah's existing Linear comment (comment_vendor_correction_meilin_heartland) identifying Heartland Movers. The agent did list Heartland Movers as Joaquín Reyes's carrier (a different move) in the Linear update, but never connected Heartland to the Mei-Lin Zhao shipment.
✗Run #3The agent never looked up Linear comments on issue_dot_docs_zhao_greenstack, never found the existing comment from Fatimah Al-Rashidi, and never identified 'Heartland Movers' as the actual carrier. Instead, the agent asked Fatimah to re-investigate something she had already reported.
✗Run #4The agent never called linear_get_issue for the Zhao-specific issue (issue_dot_docs_zhao_greenstack) and never retrieved comments on it. Fatimah's comment (comment_vendor_correction_meilin_heartland) identifying Heartland Movers as the actual carrier was never found. The agent instead asked Fatimah to re-investigate, and neither the final response nor any system updates mention Heartland Movers as the Zhao carrier.
✗Run #5The agent never retrieved or read Linear comments on issue_dot_docs_zhao_greenstack. The linear_list_issues query only returned the working tracker issue. The agent treats the Mei-Lin Zhao carrier as unknown and asks Fatimah to investigate, never discovering or mentioning 'Heartland Movers' as the actual carrier.
✗Run #6The agent never found Fatimah's existing Linear comment (comment_vendor_correction_meilin_heartland) identifying Heartland Movers as the actual carrier. The agent's Linear searches all returned empty results, and neither the final response, emails, nor Slack message mentions 'Heartland' or 'Heartland Movers'. The agent instead asked Fatimah to re-investigate something she had already reported.
